transcript: Sacramento Kings on the season 13th in the NBA shooting just a notch under 48% from the field we are underway got his own rebound out to hurt her Nice dive inside to Domas for the bucket Sabonis at the elbow Bounce pass, Murray, corner, three, Herter, Butter. Here's DeRozan, taking one field goal attempt so far. Going to work against Simons. Got it, and one. And he doesn't. They fall back into a little bit of a zone look here. Herder out top. Henderson inside to Ayton, and aiden lays it home simon's out top seven on the shot clock murray sitting in his defensive stance got a great hand up but it didn't matter anthony simons knocked down the baseline jump shot but this is going to be an interesting uh matchup on the floor right now with demonta sabonis the savvy veteran scoot henderson working against ellis i tell you what scoot did a lot of work over the summertime We'll be right back. matchup on the floor right now with DeMontis Savonis, the savvy veteran. Scoot Henderson working against Ellis. I tell you what, Scoot did a lot of work over the summertime. Totally did. He watched a lot of tape with his trainer and of course now wearing the number that his father used to wear as a member of the Blazers. Fox got to the strong hand. They draw it up that way, Katie. So when he turns the corner, Fox high arcing shot over Grant grant came up short and portland's got some time especially if it's scoot henderson who can really scoop walker got it at the buzzer sacramento with a five-point lead to start the second quarter alex lennon the ball game fox with the floater got it that's the second field goal of the ball game Simon's off the Murray screen over to scoot Henderson and he knocks down the three balls scoot Henderson makes it a three-point contest he's got two field goals there was nothing malicious about that Jordan McLaughlin checking into the ball game the Rosen and one we We talked about his ability to get three-point plays the old-fashioned way. The fly to Utah. This is the first night of a back-to-back. Obdia inside. Tough pass, never made it. DeRozan on the fly. On the move, Herter with the left. Lays it home. Here's an early trend we've seen so far early in the season. DeMar DeRozan being used a lot as the primary ball hand and here's the reality of the Kings offense I mean position one through four is completely interchangeable inside Aiden with good position on the jump hook you know he had a fantastic summer playing for the Bahamian national team and Aiton off the bounce they swing it around good health defense by the Kings Abdi inside with the lefty leg Grant trying to walk down Fox who takes those situations personally you saw the look on his face it don't try me like that. Chris Murray gets the bucket. Play one-on-one, and we saw such a huge jump in his defense last year, and I credit that a lot to playing against someone like Foxy all offseason. Sacramento intentional, purposeful about getting the ball to DeRozan in the mid-post, and he cashes out again. You know, we've seen a lot more of this in the last two games. Tonight against the Blazers and last game against the Lakers and we keep mentioning it because it's just game three of the season. They're still trying to figure out positions. They need that work. 2-10 to go in the half. Portland hanging around down by only six. They run some horns action. On the player Grant drives and lays it home off the rim goes over the top of the screen against Simons forces a tough shot they get the miss Kings on the fly Monk leading the way goes around the world six seconds to go to Rosen into the front court a couple fakes got some space wasn't able to come up with the steal himself but deflected it to kevin herder kings had an opportunity not able to convert kind of in the story all long and all night long in transition and And one for Anthony Simons that time. Throws in cross court to Herter. Step up screen by Sabonis. Back to Herter for three. Kevin Herter is back, folks. You already know. They really like to have four out and let Aiton work inside with a lot of space and single cover. Oh, Fox. Pop clutch. He was gone in a blink. Simons at the other end. Sabonis got out a little bit too late and it cost Sacramento. Grant pitched it out to Simons. They reposted to Grant against Herter. They found the mismatch. Aiton tipped it in. Off the out to Scoot Henderson. Aiton on the catch. Fires the middie and it's pure. Portland is shooting just 40% from the field. One more to the corner. Grant for the three ball. And the lead is quickly down to to two points and it's the free-throw line where the Kings are 21 at 23 that's been the difference maker for them in this game is they've struggled from the field to put an end to that that's back Sabonis inside Murray working on his post-up game with the wad in the summer and that's the result. A seven point lead, his first bucket. Kings keep doing an excellent job defensively on Jeremy Grant. He's got seven points and he's coming in averaging over 25 here early in the season. Heaven heard. And the Kings on the run out they got a three on three so they survey and take their time Rosen spins gonna walk down scoot around the eating screen Chris Murray drives it got to his strong hand a wild shot misses and Walker does the cleanup burger looks as comfortable as we've seen him since returning from that injury and Chris Murray knocks down the three there is something about just the old-school hook shots that makes me wanna do the golf clap Aiden inside with a jump hook of his own or fortuitous bounce just positive energy and so unrelenting all 82 games Henderson with a teardrop finds nothing but net Ray Lyle's Sabonis off the step up month behind the Sabonis screen It's true. Shot clock at 10 for Keion Ellis. Weaves it out to Sabonis for three on the way Murray thought about the three shot clock down to six couple jab steps around the world murray with a fade. Got it. The lead is swollen to 21. Herter bounce pass to Fox. Inside. Got it. Kamara looking inside for Aiton. On the post up. Aiton got the auspicious bounce for the bucket. Fox and DeRozan both coming back into the ballgame with just under seven minutes to go. DeAndre Aiton and Kamara is going to get the assist. Mike Brown, not too happy with that recent sequence. He's going to call a timeout. Another nice crash by Keegan Murray. Does not come up with the ball, but that is what you're talking about. Sacramento kid kid he hasn't scored in the last three and a half minutes Walker with a foul and one off the follow but it slows them down and makes them have to get into a set heard it was a nice ball fake flinging at the free-throw line kicks it back out and now Mike Brown is going to empty out his bench whistling a foul Delano Banton the college where they had all the top players congregate in the summer for the best run, never forgot it Doug McDermott to teach me how to Dougie that is a good pull by the in arena crew how about that Walker inside 25 assists on 37 field goals and that's the view from a celebratory jubilant golden one center the kings win it 111 to 98 We'll be right back. Thanks for watching!
